article_title: The Document Foundation Expels Over 30 Collabora Developers From LibreOffice Governance in a Governance Showdown Years in the Making

content_preview.title: The Document Foundation Expels Over 30 Collabora Developers From LibreOffice Governance in a Governance Showdown Years in the Making
content_preview.summary: TDF removed 30+ Collabora staff from membership—including 7 of the project's top 10 core committers—citing a legal dispute clause in newly adopted bylaws, prompting Collabora to announce a separate development track.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

The Document Foundation (TDF), the German non-profit that stewards LibreOffice, removed more than 30 Collabora staff and partners from TDF membership on April 1, 2026, stripping them of voting rights and any formal say in the project's governance. The purge included seven of LibreOffice's ten most prolific all-time core committers, among them project co-founder Michael Meeks. Collabora, the Cambridge-based company that has supplied the majority of LibreOffice's paid developers for o...

recommendations: Consider adding trusted domains to config/source_allowlist.txt

editor_notes.content_quality: Excellent governance controversy piece with rigorous sourcing from both sides. Uses primary sources (TDF blog posts, Collabora blog) alongside secondary reporting (Slashdot, The Register). The article correctly attributes competing characterizations without taking sides. Analysis section places the conflict in historical context (2010 fork) effectively. Length (1,099 words) appropriate for News category.
editor_notes.source_verification: All 6 sources fetched and verified. Slashdot: confirms 30+ removed including all named individuals (Behrens, Holesovsky, Engelhard, McNamara, Meeks, Nouws, Vignoli), Community Bylaws legal dispute clause, membership revocation ≠ ban from contributing. The Register: confirms CODA release November 2025, LibreOffice Online revival March 2026, Meeks 'extraordinary' characterization and 'unclear what more we could give' quote, Paolo Vecchi 'level playing field' quote. TDF official post: confirms failed audits 2023 (raised concerns) and 2024 (confirmed), governance violations (preferential trademark licensing, development contracts to board members' companies), structural reforms enacted. Collabora post: confirms all Meeks quotes ('unproven legal concerns', 'retrospective rule-making by a political faction'), own Gerrit infrastructure announcement, new product/Classic product plans, 'no longer makes much sense' quote. TDF Q&A: confirms exact patch statistics (TDF 4,077/37%, Collabora 4,763/43%, volunteers 1,871/17%), hiring plans. TDF open letter: confirms 'personal conduct was not the issue', open to all developers, governance structure issue.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: All claims verified against primary sources. The 'loaded language' automated warning flags Michael Meeks' direct quote containing the word 'clearly' — this is a verbatim attributed quote from the Collabora post, not editorial language. No hallucinations detected.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and professional throughout. The article presents TDF's governance rationale and Collabora's objections with equal weight. No editorializing in the Analysis section beyond what's supported by the sourced facts.
editor_notes.originality: New story not previously covered in The Machine Herald. Significant open-source governance event with broad implications for LibreOffice's sustainability.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: High-quality governance controversy piece with primary sourcing from both parties. All claims verified. The 'loaded language' automated flag is a false positive from a verbatim attributed quote. Approved.
